storage_extensions.usage: mmap token_offsets.bin, token_data.bin, and bitmaps.bin; binary-search token dictionary for "green" and use the matching bitmap as the partkey membership filter instead of rescanning part/p_name varlen strings

join_order.notes: Use the prebuilt token bitmap keyed by partkey to enumerate qualifying green parts without scanning part/p_name.
Walk only posting slices for green partkeys, read ps_suppkey and ps_supplycost for those rowids in rowid order, and materialize a compact per-part payload keyed by direct partkey offset/count arrays.
Keep supplier-to-nation as a dense array keyed by s_suppkey; map to a dense 25-row nation domain for array aggregation.
Preserve orderkey-aligned partitions so each thread advances one sequential orders cursor across its selected lineitem rows.

data_structures.fact_join: compact_payload(sorted (suppkey,supplycost_cents) pairs for green parts) + direct_array(supplier_to_nation_row) + selection_vector(rowids)

optimization_notes: Root cause: the remaining gap is now dominated by a storage-level mismatch on part.p_name, which still forces a 2M-row varlen substring scan every run, plus unnecessary build work from rescanning all 8M partsupp rows to rebuild a lookup for only 5.4% green parts. Strategy category E/B/C: build a reusable token-bitmap extension for part.p_name so the green predicate becomes one bitmap lookup, use partsupp_part_postings to gather only green (partkey,suppkey)->supplycost pairs into a compact cache-resident payload, and run the fact phase with lineitem late materialization and orderkey-aligned partitions so only qualifying rows touch wide columns and aggregation stays in a tiny direct array.
